So total leads, the way that you can collect this, I think the most accurate way to do it is to go to your landing page. The other option, whatever option you choose, just be consistent with it so that the stats don't vary. Option two is you can go to whatever your lead sheet is that your leads are going into or if you have software that the leads are going into and you want to make sure that the doubles aren't counted or you consistently count the doubles because it almost always happens that people submit to things twice, etc. So the thing is because my reasoning was I didn't want to add more work to myself. I included bum leads because I figure those percentages are constant and I just didn't want to have more work for no reason. So I just assumed all of those things were just unchanging from campaign to campaign. Total leads that came in through my funnel and you can count that on your lead sheet or through your ClickFunnels page, just click contacts and then you can set the date range. So that is the easy way of doing it for leads. I don't like counting off of conversions because those pixels are notoriously flawed. So I would count it off the actual names and numbers that I collected. Total ad spend, you want to look at what have I spent in over the 30 day period. So this is, most of these are relatively self-explanatory. I think you'd know where to find them. Percentage of people who stayed past the front end into an EFT basically stack up the contracts and count them and divide that by how many people started. Exits that should be, that's why I like this as a weekly thing because sometimes you don't know if you're going to collect on a decline payment or if you're going to recover someone and so I think that's done better on a weekly basis. It's like who did we lose this week from billing? And so you can either report that daily or you can report it weekly. And the average of people who make it off the front end, if you're doing a bribe offer then it should be over 80%. If you're doing a standard front end play and then flipping them then it would be closer to 65%. Okay. Attrition and churn, average gyms that work with us do about seven. That's not saying that that's good. It's just saying that that's below the industry average, industry average is 10. So I would want everyone who's watching this to do below seven definitely where if you want to, like for me, for me to tell you that your retention is not the problem, you need to be below three. So it means that your fulfillment is an issue until you get below 3%. Just letting you know. Five ish percent is kind of an average here. So I would keep that as your baseline of like if we're above that then we need to improve on our fulfillment in our sales. Just sell with more conviction. Okay. Profit, the average gym that works with us does 8,900 a month in profit. That was pulled from a survey. I rounded it but that was pulled from a survey, the most recent survey we ran. And then margin percentage, about 25% for our guys. Now the industry average, you've probably heard me say this is 8 to 12%. So if you're new then that might be where you're going to be and we're going to improve that and that's why you're here. To do all of this stuff, I can't have averages for you because a lot of you are on your life up to this point. So I hope this makes sense in terms of how to use this. Please feel free to cut, paste, move things around, report different things on a daily weekly basis, etc. But these are all the stats that inform what is not going well with your business and what you need to do. And it's usually the percentages are the things that you want to increase to make and that's why the percentages are the benchmarks, not the absolute numbers. So that's what I focus on with my teams. I never gave sales numbers, I gave percentage numbers because things are dynamic within a business but the effort can be constant. So I know that we should be converting X% of our leads, we should be getting X% to show, X% to close, etc. Rather than saying I need you to sell 10 a week. Well I need you to sell this percentage, secure it. And so that concludes the where you can track your metrics and where to find them. Last bit, your team should be doing as much of this as possible over time so that you can just come in here and look at it. And I will say it again, every week you should copy all the info and put it onto a separate Google Doc that only you can see so that's kind of like your record yourself from it.
